endpoints.name: Get Card type Information for Visa, Mastercard, and Discover
endpoints.description: Gets Card type information for Visa, Mastercard, and Discover. Useful if you prohibit or allow certain activity based on card type. For example, you may not want to allow a subscription to be created using a prepaid card.

body.merchant_id: <Provide merchant id that links to API Key>

endpoints.name: Authorize Transaction
endpoints.description: Authorizes a credit card for capture at a later time. An authorized transaction will continue to be open until it expires or a capture message is received. Authorizations are automatically voided if they are not captured within 28 days, although most issuing banks will release the hold after 24 hours in retail environments or 7 days in card not present environments.

body.merchant_id: <Provide merchant id that links to API Key>

endpoints.name: Close Batch
endpoints.description: Closes a batch. Use this request when the timing of the batch close needs to be controlled rather than relying on the once-daily automatic batch close which is 9 PM Pacific by default, and can be changed in the Qualpay Manager administration settings.

body.merchant_id: <Provide merchant id that links to API Key>

endpoints.name: Capture an Authorized Transaction
endpoints.description: Captures an authorized transaction for any amount up to the amount originally authorized. An authorized transaction can only be captured once.

body.merchant_id: <Provide merchant id that links to API Key>

endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{PGIDORIG}} with a real pgidorig.

endpoints.name: Issue Credit to Cardholder
endpoints.description: Issues an unlinked credit. Credit requests require that the cardholder data is  provided in the request. Credits are only available during the first 30 days of account opening unless you contact Qualpay support to make other arrangements. The refund request should generally be used to return money to the cardholder, as it is a reversal of a previously captured transaction. A refund request is linked to the original transaction which is helpful for reconciliation purposes.

body.merchant_id: <Provide merchant id that links to API Key>

endpoints.name: Send Transaction Receipt Email
endpoints.description: Sends the transaction receipt to multiple email addresses. Receipts can be sent only for successful transactions.

body.merchant_id: <Provide merchant id that links to API Key>

endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{PGID}} with a real pgid.

endpoints.name: Force Transaction Approval
endpoints.description: Forces an approval, used when an online authorization request received a 'declined' reason code and you have received an authorization from a voice or automated response (ARU) system. The required fields are the same as a sale or authorization request, except that the expiration date (exp_date) is not required, and the 6-character authorization code (auth_code) is required.

body.merchant_id: <Provide merchant id that links to API Key>

endpoints.name: Recharge Previously Settled Transaction
endpoints.description: Creates a new sale transaction using the cardholder data from a previous successful transaction.

body.merchant_id: <Provide merchant id that links to API Key>

endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{PGIDORIG}} with a real pgidorig.

endpoints.name: Refund Previously Captured Transaction
endpoints.description: Returns money to the cardholder from a previously captured transaction. Multiple refunds are allowed per captured transaction, provided that the sum of all refunds does not exceed the original captured transaction amount. Authorizations that have not been captured are not eligible for a refund.

body.merchant_id: <Provide merchant id that links to API Key>

endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{PGIDORIG}} with a real pgidorig.

endpoints.name: Sale (Auth + Capture)
endpoints.description: Requests authorization, and, if approved, will immediately capture the transaction to be included in the next batch close. This transaction type is used in card-present environments, and also card-not-present environments where no physical goods are being shipped.

body.merchant_id: <Provide merchant id that links to API Key>

endpoints.name: Tokenize Card
endpoints.description: Once stored, a unique card_id is returned for use in future transactions. Optionally, tokenization can be requested in an authorization, verify, force, credit, or sale request by sending the tokenize field set to true.

body.merchant_id: <Provide merchant id that links to API Key>

endpoints.name: Verify Card
endpoints.description: A verify request will return success if the cardholder information was verified by the issuer. If AVS or CVV data is included in the message, then the AVS or CVV result code will be returned in the response message. This is useful if you want to determine if you have been presented with a valid card, but are not ready to authorize the card.

body.merchant_id: <Provide merchant id that links to API Key>

endpoints.name: Void a Previously Authorized Transaction
endpoints.description: Authorizations can be voided at any time until Qualpay automatically voids them at 28 days. Captured transactions can be voided until the batch is closed. If your batch closes and you did not void the transaction in time, you may make a refund request.

body.merchant_id: <Provide merchant id that links to API Key>

endpoints.notes: No authentication required. Replace {{PGIDORIG}} with a real pgidorig.
